claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 9 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 5 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 9 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let me start with: **Do you have any children that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you tell me how many children you'd like to inquire about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have children I'm responsible for. I have two children - Alex, who's 9, and Blake, who's 5. I'd like to find out if I'm eligible to claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's great that you want to check your eligibility for Alex (age 9) and Blake (age 5).

Let me ask you some questions to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for both children.

First, let me confirm: **Do both Alex and Blake currently live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - both Alex and Blake live at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, thank you. Now I need to ask: **Are you or your partner responsible for both Alex and Blake?** By this I mean, do you (or your partner if you have one) have parental responsibility and provide for their upbringing - such as providing food, clothing, and a place to live?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm responsible for both of them - I provide everything they need, their food, clothes, and of course they live here with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent. Now I need to ask about your own circumstances: **Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I have a few more questions to complete the assessment.

**Are either Alex or Blake currently in any of the following situations:**
- In hospital or care for more than 12 weeks?
- Living with someone else (even temporarily)?
- In full-time education or training?

For Blake at age 5, this likely doesn't apply, but for Alex at age 9, I just want to check if there are any special circumstances I should be aware of.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nothing like that. Both Alex and Blake are just living at home with me as normal. Alex goes to primary school, and Blake does too - just the regular school day, nothing special or full-time in the sense you're talking about.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for clarifying. Regular school attendance is absolutely fine and doesn't affect eligibility.

Just a couple more questions:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake?** For example, another parent or family member?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them. It's just me looking after them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you. Just one final question to complete the assessment:

**Are you subject to immigration control, or do you have any restrictions on your right to reside in the UK?** In other words, do you have the right to live and claim benefits in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't have any restrictions - I have the right to live here and claim benefits.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent news! **You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.**

Here's a summary of the assessment:

**For Alex (age 9):**
- ✓ Under 16 years old
- ✓ Lives with you
- ✓ You're responsible for their upbringing
- ✓ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- ✓ You live in the UK with the right to claim benefits

**For Blake (age 5):**
- ✓ Under 16 years old
- ✓ Lives with you
- ✓ You're responsible for their upbringing
- ✓ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake
- ✓ You live in the UK with the right to claim benefits

Both children meet all the eligibility criteria. You can proceed with your Child Benefit claim for both Alex and Blake. 

**Important note:** While you are eligible to claim Child Benefit, if you or your partner have an individual income over £50,000, you may need to pay a tax charge (the High Income Child Benefit Charge). However, this doesn't affect your eligibility to claim - you can still claim even if this applies to you.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 9 years old, which is under 16, so meets the age requirement. Alex lives with you, you are responsible for Alex and provide for their upbringing, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and you live in the UK with the right to claim benefits. Alex attends regular primary school which does not affect eligibility. Therefore, you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 5 years old, which is under 16, so meets the age requirement. Blake lives with you, you are responsible for Blake and provide for their upbringing, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake, and you live in the UK with the right to claim benefits. Blake attends regular primary school which does not affect eligibility. Therefore, you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is responsible for two children, Alex (age 9) and Blake (age 5), who both live with them in the UK. Both children are under 16 years old, the claimant provides for their upbringing, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for either child, and the claimant has the right to live and claim benefits in the UK. All eligibility criteria are met for both children.

Blake attends regular primary school which does not affect eligibility. Therefore, you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake.
